heading: Homeowner Assistance Fund
label: 9058d Homeowner Assistance Fund
notes: CodificationSection was enacted as part of the American Rescue Plan Act of 2021, and not as part of the CARES Act which in part comprises this chapter.

children.heading: Appropriation
children.label: a Appropriation
children.text: In addition to amounts otherwise available, there is appropriated to the Secretary of the Treasury for the Homeowner Assistance Fund established under subsection (c) for fiscal year 2021, out of any money in the Treasury not otherwise appropriated, $9,961,000,000, to remain available until , for qualified expenses that meet the purposes specified under subsection (c) and expenses described in subsection (d)(1).September 30, 2025

children.heading: Conforming loan limit
children.label: 1 Conforming loan limit
children.text: The term “conforming loan limit” means the applicable limitation governing the maximum original principal obligation of a mortgage secured by a single-family residence, a mortgage secured by a 2-family residence, a mortgage secured by a 3-family residence, or a mortgage secured by a 4-family residence, as determined and adjusted annually under and .section 1717(b)(2) of title 12section 1454(a)(2) of title 12

children.heading: Dwelling
children.label: 2 Dwelling
children.text: The term “dwelling” means any building, structure, or portion thereof which is occupied as, or designed or intended for occupancy as, a residence by one or more individuals.

children.label: A
children.text: a State; or

children.label: B
children.text: any entity eligible for payment under subsection (f).

children.label: A
children.text: that is secured by a mortgage, deed of trust, or other consensual security interest on a principal residence of a borrower that is (i) a 1- to 4-unit dwelling, or (ii) residential real property that includes a 1- to 4-unit dwelling; and

children.label: B
children.text: the unpaid principal balance of which was, at the time of origination, not more than the conforming loan limit.

children.heading: Fund
children.label: 5 Fund
children.text: The term “Fund” means the Homeowner Assistance Fund established under subsection (c).

children.heading: Secretary
children.label: 6 Secretary
children.text: The term “Secretary” means the Secretary of the Treasury.

children.heading: State
children.label: 7 State
children.text: The term “State” means any State of the United States, the District of Columbia, the Commonwealth of Puerto Rico, Guam, American Samoa, the United States Virgin Islands, and the Commonwealth of the Northern Mariana Islands.

children.label: A
children.text: mortgage payment assistance;

children.label: B
children.text: financial assistance to allow a homeowner to reinstate a mortgage or to pay other housing related costs related to a period of forbearance, delinquency, or default;

children.label: C
children.text: principal reduction;

children.label: D
children.text: facilitating interest rate reductions;

children.label: i
children.text: utilities, including electric, gas, home energy, and water;

children.label: ii
children.text: internet service, including broadband internet access service, as defined in section 8.1(b) of title 47, Code of Federal Regulations (or any successor regulation);

children.label: iii
children.text: homeowner’s insurance, flood insurance, and mortgage insurance; and

children.label: iv
children.text: homeowner’s association, condominium association fees, or common charges;

children.label: F
children.text: reimbursement of funds expended by a State, local government, or designated entity under subsection (f) during the period beginning on , and ending on the date that the first funds are disbursed by the eligible entity under the Homeowner Assistance Fund, for the purpose of providing housing or utility payment assistance to homeowners or otherwise providing funds to prevent foreclosure or post-foreclosure eviction of a homeowner or prevent mortgage delinquency or loss of housing or utilities as a response to the coronavirus disease (COVID) pandemic; andJanuary 21, 2020

children.label: G
children.text: any other assistance to promote housing stability for homeowners, including preventing mortgage delinquency, default, foreclosure, post-foreclosure eviction of a homeowner, or the loss of utility or home energy services, as determined by the Secretary.

children.heading: Targeting
children.label: 2 Targeting
children.text: Not less than 60 percent of amounts made to each eligible entity allocated amounts under subsection (d) or (f) shall be used for qualified expenses that assist homeowners having incomes equal to or less than 100 percent of the area median income for their household size or equal to or less than 100 percent of the median income for the United States, as determined by the Secretary of Housing and Urban Development, whichever is greater. The eligible entity shall prioritize remaining funds to socially disadvantaged individuals.

children.label: A
children.text: to the Department of the Treasury, an amount not to exceed $40,000,000 to administer and oversee the Fund, and to provide technical assistance to eligible entities for the creation and implementation of State and tribal programs to administer assistance from the Fund; and

children.label: B
children.text: to the Inspector General of the Department of the Treasury, an amount to not exceed $2,600,000 for oversight of the program under this section.

children.label: A
children.text: the average number of unemployed individuals measured over a period of time not fewer than 3 months and not more than 12 months; and

children.label: i
children.text: mortgage payments that are more than 30 days past due; or

children.label: ii
children.text: mortgages in foreclosure.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Each State of the United States, the District of Columbia, and the Commonwealth of Puerto Rico shall receive no less than $50,000,000 for the purposes established in (c) .1So in original. Probably should be preceded by “subsection”.1

children.heading: Pro rata adjustments
children.label: B Pro rata adjustments
children.text: The Secretary shall adjust on a pro rata basis the amount of the payments for each State of the United States, the District of Columbia, and the Commonwealth of Puerto Rico determined under this subsection without regard to this subparagraph to the extent necessary to comply with the requirements of subparagraph (A).

children.heading: Territory set-aside
children.label: 4 Territory set-aside
children.text: Notwithstanding any other provision of this section, of the amounts appropriated under subsection (a), the Secretary shall reserve $30,000,000 to be disbursed to Guam, American Samoa, the United States Virgin Islands, and the Commonwealth of the Northern Mariana Islands based on each such territory’s share of the combined total population of all such territories, as determined by the Secretary. For the purposes of this paragraph, population shall be determined based on the most recent year for which data are available from the United States Census Bureau.

children.heading: Tribal set-aside
children.label: 5 Tribal set-aside
children.text: The Secretary shall allocate funds to any eligible entity designated under subsection (f) pursuant to the requirements of that subsection.

children.heading: Distribution of funds to States
children.label: e Distribution of funds to States

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The Secretary shall make payments, beginning not later than 45 days after , from amounts allocated under subsection (d) to eligible entities that have notified the Secretary that they request to receive payment from the Fund and that the eligible entity will use such payments in compliance with this section.March 11, 2021

If a State does not request allocated funds by the 45th day after , such State shall not be eligible for a payment from the Secretary pursuant to this section, and the Secretary shall, by the 180th day after , reallocate any funds that were not requested by such State among the States that have requested funds by the 45th day after . For any such reallocation of funds, the Secretary shall adhere to the requirements of subsection (d), except for paragraph (1), to the greatest extent possible, provided that the Secretary shall also take into consideration in determining such reallocation a State’s remaining need and a State’s record of using payments from the Fund to serve homeowners at disproportionate risk of mortgage default, foreclosure, or displacement, including homeowners having incomes equal to or less than 100 percent of the area median income for their household size or 100 percent of the median income for the United States, as determined by the Secretary of Housing and Urban Development, whichever is greater, and minority homeowners.March 11, 2021March 11, 2021March 11, 2021

children.heading: Set-aside
children.label: 1 Set-aside
children.text: Notwithstanding any other provision of this section, of the amounts appropriated under subsection (a), the Secretary shall use 5 percent to make payments to entities that are eligible for payments under clauses (i) and (ii) of for the purposes described in subsection (c).section 9058a(b)(2)(A) of this title

children.heading: Allocation and payment
children.label: 2 Allocation and payment
children.text: The Secretary shall allocate the funds set aside under paragraph (1) using the allocation formulas described in clauses (i) and (ii) of , and shall make payments of such amounts beginning no later than 45 days after , to entities eligible for payment under clauses (i) and (ii) of that notify the Secretary that they request to receive payments allocated from the Fund by the Secretary for purposes described under subsection (c) and will use such payments in compliance with this section.section 9058a(b)(2)(A) of this titlesection 9058a(b)(2)(A) of this titleMarch 11, 2021

children.heading: Adjustment
children.label: 3 Adjustment
children.text: Allocations provided under this subsection may be further adjusted as provided by .section 9058a(b)(2)(B) of this title
